question: Is the grand piano positioned in the center-left area of the stage, aligning with the red region of the segmentation map?
Are audience chairs placed on the right side of the stage, aligning with the blue region of the segmentation map?
Are curtains visible in the upper background area, corresponding to the purple region of the segmentation map?
Are columns present on both the far left and far right sides of the stage, corresponding to the gray regions of the segmentation map?
Is a lighting rig or truss visible at the top edge of the image, corresponding to the yellow region of the segmentation map?
Is there a curved or arched backdrop element behind the stage, corresponding to the white region of the segmentation map?
Is the main subject clearly a grand piano with its lid propped open, preserving the pose and silhouette of the reference image?
Are the piano's defining details—specifically the keyboard keys and the internal gold frame/strings—recognizable despite the stylized rendering?
Does the image utilize a Fauvist color palette with intense, non-naturalistic colors (e.g., a purple piano or red floor) as requested?
Does the image feature thick, visible, expressive brushstrokes and impasto textures consistent with the provided Fauvist style reference?
Are the piano's reflections and material properties reinterpreted as bold, simplified blocks of color rather than photorealistic glossy highlights?
Is the image free from severe geometric distortions, such as melting piano legs, floating chairs, or incoherent keyboard structure?
